purpose: add_fact_from_csv's ragged-row arms: a row WIDER than the first row fails the read wholesale — ValueError 'Error reading CSV file <path>: Error tokenizing data. C error: Expected 5 fields in line 2, saw 6\n' (the pandas C-tokenizer text, line = record ordinal; the repo-resolved path is identical for both engines run from this repo), loading nothing from that file; a wide FIRST row instead fixes the field count — its extra trailing column is silently ignored (rgf_a loads with its 5 leading cells) and later narrower rows pad with empty cells (rgf_b loads normally; rgf_c's 2-field row takes start/end 0 and shows only in the t=0 frame) — the whole-file vs silently-widened asymmetry turns on which row is widest.
provenance: docs/surface.md fn:add_fact_from_csv ragged classes; pyreason.py:1331-1341 at the pin (read_csv header=None, dtype=str, keep_default_na=False); screened live 2026-07-12 (4 fresh pin processes, digest-stable); fixtures harness/fixtures/facts-ragged-wide.csv, facts-ragged-first-wide.csv
